Article.ArticleTitle: Hands that Heal: The Role of Gentle Massage in Managing Hyperbilirubinemia Among Saudi Neonates: Randomized Controlled Experimental Study.

Abstract.AbstractText: Neonatal hyperbilirubinemia is common, with phototherapy as the standard treatment. Gentle massage therapy is being explored as a complementary approach for its benefits on circulation and digestion.
To evaluate the impact of gentle massage therapy on bilirubin reduction in neonates with hyperbilirubinemia in Saudi Arabia.
The research group included 30 neonates who received gentle massage therapy alongside phototherapy. Bilirubin levels were measured at baseline and during the intervention.

Most of the neonates in the research group were discharged on the third day, gentle massage reduced recovery time from hyperbilirubinemia by 0.698 days, with males recovering faster and 35-week neonates taking longer than 36-week neonates.

Gentle massage therapy is safe and effective for managing hyperbilirubinemia. It reduces hospital stays and improves neonatal care. Further research is recommended to standardize protocols and train caregivers.
Abstract.CopyrightInformation: Copyright: © 2025 Journal of Pharmacy and Bioallied Sciences.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Nursing Specialist, NICO, King Fahad Medical City Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.
Nursing Administration and Education Department, College of Nursing, King Saud University, Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: There are no conflicts of interest.
